subject: Accounting
title: The General Journal
description: A 60-second lesson on The General Journal.

hook.text: The general journal is the company's diary—every financial event recorded in date order.

buildup.text: Each entry includes the date, accounts affected, amounts, and a brief description.

discovery.text: The journal is the first place transactions are recorded—before they move to the ledger.

twist.text: In modern software, journal entry and ledger posting happen simultaneously—but the logic is the same.

climax.text: A well-maintained journal creates a complete, chronological trail of every financial event.

punchline.text: The journal tells the story of money, day by day.

quiz.question: What is the general journal?
options: A chronological record of all financial transactions
